items.title: Walid Haniyeh, deputy Nukhba company commander, eliminated in Gaza
items.desc: IDF and ISA eliminate Walid Haniyeh, Deputy Commander of an elite Nukhba company, who commanded a terror cell infiltrating Israel during the October 7th massacre.

items.title: KMC starts process to suspend Kolkata ex-mayor’s OSD
items.desc: Kolkata Municipal Corporation begins suspension process for senior official Kalicharan Banerjee after arrest in Taratala warehouse collapse case and illegal plan probe.

items.title: BJP trade union files memo against Hakim, ex-councillors
items.desc: BJP-affiliated Bharatiya Janata Majdoor Cell filed a memo at Taratala police seeking FIR and arrest of ex-mayor Firhad Hakim, two ex-councillors over illegal construction linked to warehouse collapse.

items.title: Caste-on-ID Card Row: DMK, DK Criticise; Ministers Clarify
items.desc: Tamil Nadu govt clarifies school ID cards will include blood group and Aadhaar, not caste, after DMK and DK criticise minister’s earlier remarks.

items.title: The most widely consumed beverage on Earth outside of water and tea originally contained cocaine — formulated in 1886 by
items.desc: The 1886 American pharmaceutical landscape into which Coca-Cola was introduced bore essentially no resemblance to the regulated drug-approval system that has governed American medicine since the passage of the Pure Food and Drug Act in 1906 and the establishm…

items.title: CNN Table Explodes as Abby Phillip Tries To Explain The Difference Between Antisemitism and Criticism of Israel
items.desc:
CNN's Table For Five exploded Saturday as host Abby Phillip attempted to explain the difference between blatant antisemitism and everyday criticism of Israel.
The post CNN Table Explodes as Abby Phillip Tries To Explain The Difference Between Antisemitism and…

items.title: Hit by ebola, conflict & curbs, DR Congo’s WC show unites, gives hope to embattled nation
items.desc: DR Congo’s first World Cup since 1974 unites fans amid Ebola, conflict and travel curbs, boosting hope as the Leopards fight to reach knockouts.

items.title: Manickam Tagore appointed new TNCC chief
items.desc: AICC chief Mallikarjun Kharge appoints Virudhunagar MP B Manickam Tagore as new TNCC president, replacing K Selvaperunthagai after his request to be relieved.

items.title: Mohamed Salah Suffers Minor Hamstring Strain in Egypt’s Draw with Iran
items.desc: Coach Hossam Hassan is sounding positive about Salah’s chances of facing Australia in the Round of 32 despite the knock.

items.title: Collector urges farmers to avail land parcel premium
items.desc: Nashik Ring Road land acquisition: Collector urges farmers to consent early to secure 25% premium; joint bank accounts proposed for undivided heir lands.

items.title: CCTV video shows SI slapping 2 women at northwest Delhi hotel
items.desc: A shocking CCTV video from Adarsh Nagar, Delhi, allegedly shows a sub-inspector slapping two women during a hotel intervention. Police were called to a late-night clash, and the women claim the officer assaulted them in front of a female colleague. The SI has…

items.title: India-China border trade via Lipulekh yet to take off despite June 1 reopening
items.desc: India-China border trade via Lipulekh Pass reopened June 1 after six years, but remains stalled due to customs delays, permits and transport issues in Pithoragarh.

items.title: Govt okays 192 staff posts for 2 state-run hospitals
items.desc: Maharashtra govt sanctions 192 staff posts for two nearing-completion state hospitals in Nashik, including a 105-bed referral hospital and 100-bed women’s hospital.

items.title: Lebanon's deal with Israel requires Hezbollah to disarm. That might be difficult
items.desc: Lebanon and Israel signed the deal in Washington on Friday without Hezbollah. The agreement links Israel's withdrawal from Lebanon to the Iran-backed militant group's disarmament, something Hezbollah rejects.

items.title: Man hospitalised after putting dead snake in mouth for reel
items.desc: Man in Bijnor, UP hospitalised after repeatedly putting a dead snake in his mouth while filming a social media reel; doctor warns against risky stunts.

items.title: 4 jailed Nihangs granted bail amid hopes of standoff easing
items.desc: Chamoli court grants bail to four Nihang Sikhs jailed in Karnaprayag assault case, raising hopes of easing Uttarakhand standoff; police remain on alert.

items.title: Trump unloads on 'lunatic' John Bolton after ex-aide pleads guilty in classified docs case
items.desc: Trump mocks John Bolton on Truth Social after his former national security adviser pleads guilty to unauthorized possession of classified documents.

items.title: LS Speaker, Maha CM to attend Ahilyabai Holkar birth anniversary event
items.desc: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla and Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis to attend Ahilyabai Holkar’s 301st birth anniversary event in Agra on June 30.

items.title: Woman possesses ganja worth over Rs2L, held
items.desc: Panchavati police arrested a 46-year-old woman with 10 kg ganja worth Rs 2.1 lakh, seized goods worth Rs 70,000; NDPS case filed, prior cases noted.

items.title: 2 Delhi youths swept away in Rishikesh
items.desc: Two Delhi youths, Prem Singh and Rajan Kumar, went missing after being swept away by strong Ganga currents in Rishikesh; SDRF search continues.
